scope_note: QA Fibonacci Frobenius Character cert [394]. Primary sources: Ireland & Rosen (1990) ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.5 (Legendre symbol, quadratic reciprocity) and Ch.3 (Fermat-Euler criterion); Wall (1960) doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Fibonacci mod p, entry point alpha(p), pi(p) period); Neukirch (1999) ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8 §I.8-9 (Frobenius element, Artin map for quadratic extensions); Serre (1973) ISBN 978-0-387-90041-7 §I.2 (GL_1 Artin representation, Hecke Grossencharacter). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for all primes p != 5, the e-component of sigma^p(1,0) satisfies sigma^p(1,0)[e] == (5/p) mod p, where (5/p) is the Legendre symbol (+1 if p splits in Z[phi], -1 if inert, 0 if p=5 ramified). Equivalently F_p == (5/p) mod p. This is the GL_1 Frobenius character: the p-th iterate of the QA sigma-operator encodes the Frobenius element of Gal(Q(sqrt(5))/Q) at p as a computed orbit value -- no table lookup. Checks: (C1) F_p == (5/p) mod p for all primes p <= 500; (C2) split primes (p==+-1 mod 5) give F_p == +1; (C3) inert primes (p==+-2 mod 5) give F_p == -1; (C4) ramified prime p=5 gives F_5 == 0; (C5) F_p is computed by iterating sigma exactly p times from (b,e)=(1,0) -- no closed-form bypass. Theorem NT: arithmetic is exact integer (Fraction-free); sigma step is discrete; the mod-p reduction is a terminal observer projection. Extends cert [386] (ring-theoretic inert/split/ramified classification) and cert [391] (sigma IS phi-multiplication on Z[phi]); bridges to cert [390] (GL_2 HMF Galois symmetry) by supplying the GL_1 Frobenius character that the GL_2 story extends. Derived 2026-06-12.
